## [00:00 - 03:03]

In this lesson, I'm going to be unpacking exactly how to navigate the world of YouTube influences using an example from a friend's brand. Throughout this module, we've spoken about using micro Instagram influences at scale, doing a Facebook ads takeover, and we've covered tactics that are applicable to YouTube, but it's really important here to cover YouTube in some more detail, as this is a powerful channel that we want to be taking advantage of. If we don't focus on YouTube influences, we're missing out on an incredible SEO tactic with evergreen results as well as a great new sales channel. So in this lesson, I'm going to be using as a case study one of my good friends, Brandon and Justin's brand called Chinaama. It's a car detailing product, which is a ceramic waterless product that cleans, seals and protects, and the brand is doing eight figures in revenue annually now. And I actually met Brandon and Justin through Start and Scale. When we first launched the course, we ran a Start and Scale competition for the fastest growing brand, and Brandon and Justin actually ended up winning it. So we flew them from the US to Melbourne, and we've stayed in close contact ever since. More recently, I just spoke at a mastermind with them in Thailand, so we're all really close. And at the mastermind, they were telling me all about what they're doing on YouTube. So when I started thinking about this lesson, I thought, who better to have a chat with them then? So let's get into it now. First off, we spoke about the benefits of YouTube influences or the influences on other channels. The most obvious benefit of YouTube is that it's evergreen. While Instagram's only relevant for a few days or a few weeks maximum, YouTube can be relevant for years. So when considering spend on YouTube, Shinarma definitely sees YouTube as a longer term play, so they don't mind as much if they break even on a collaboration over the first couple of weeks or even the first month, as the results will truly show over time. Then of course, there's the SEO benefits. Often YouTube videos will be the first organic listing to appear in Google, not to mention that 8 out of 10 Google video search results are YouTube videos. Key words are really important on YouTube, especially in the video's title. A good way to get started on YouTube will always be to get some videos done by nano or micro influences with your brand name and then words like review, most recommended, as well as how to and tutorial focus content. So for example, Shinarma review. Then you want to move on to some how to or trend focus content. Another good title that Shinarma stumbled across by accident was the truth about Shinarma. Then you want to start to do some industry keyword research and find all of the videos that have performed the best across your industry or category and start to create content with influences around these terms or even for your own channel down the track. ## [03:03 - 06:06]

resources section. Otherwise, I'd simply suggest using YouTube search suggest feature, which will help pre-fill a search based off the words that you type in. Another advantage of YouTube is the conversion rate of YouTube influences. Like we spoke about in our lesson, I identifying the influential. It's the YouTubers relationship with their audience that's going to move the needle most on whether they're a converter or a content creator. YouTubers have a high level of authenticity from putting themselves out there consistently again and again. Their audience really feels like they know and trust them and this is why they convert the best out of any other platform to date. The most popular kinds of YouTube videos are going to be YouTube product review or product placement with larger influences. This is because a lot of larger influences once they get to a certain size aren't willing to do an entire video dedicated to your brand. Then you can also do joint giveaways, product tutorials and how to's, unboxing videos and then there are trending YouTube styles like ASMR or mukbang. So even though there are huge upsides to working with YouTube influences, the downsides are definitely when it comes to cost. Influencers are aware of their value and will want to be paid accordingly as well as the time consuming nature of influence and discovery and outreach. Here are several strategies that the YouTube pros have in place to reduce the cost and time spent around YouTube influences. So let's start off with cost. Instead of offering an upfront payment, try to pay via an affiliate commission code. There are a few platforms that can help you with these payouts so I'll put some links in the resources section for you below. However, a lot of the time YouTube is a more interested in upfront payment rather than affiliate code, but just make sure that you negotiate on this one. I'd say that it does depend on the size of the influencer here, especially if they have an agent or in hot demand. So be a little careful going in to harder negotiations with really large category influences so as not to put them off. Then there's the other option of negotiating longer term agreements. Even if you're not entirely sure that you want to enter into a longer term agreement with this influencer, it can be a really great way to negotiate. Right now, Sharnam is finding the most success doing four week deals with one video a week to their audience over the four week period. ## [06:06 - 09:10]

around manually finding the right influences to work with. For one, Sharnam is a very niche product, so only certain kinds of influences are going to be relevant to speak about their product. Luckily for them, car videos are prolific on YouTube. The main manual method to finding influences is via keyword search and by finding influences who have done videos for other brands in your category that aren't necessarily competing brands. Sometimes channels feature other channels, but a lot of the time they don't. So there's no similar to profile section, but there's of course a similar videos section in the right hand side bar, which is called Up Next. And when you finish one video, YouTube will often go right ahead and play another. There are also often featured lists of influences on Google for YouTubers, but these will be far larger YouTubers. So manual search by keywords is going to be the key. At Sharnamma, they have two full-time VA's going through list building, pulling the YouTuber's emails and keeping track of who they're reaching out to. So moving on to the outreach itself, it's pretty fine to use a pretty standard template here that basically goes, hey, we love your channel. We have a product that's X. When it comes to briefing the influencer on what to do, for a long time, Sharnamma really left it up to the YouTuber, but they've realized now that they know what works. But right now, Sharnamma's simply been getting them to focus on the three main features of the product. So for Sharnamma, that's how it cleans, shines and protects. Then they leave it entirely up to the influencer on their creative side. So however the influencer wants to position and style it. At the end of the day, the YouTuber knows their audience the best, but that doesn't necessarily mean that they know what will sell your product the best. So give them a couple of talking points and let them do the rest. So if the YouTuber isn't that into it, they're going to see straight through it. In terms of tracking for YouTube collaborations, another huge pro on YouTube is that you can simply add a trackable link or a link straight from your commission tracking platform to YouTube videos. Make sure this is always the first link or at least always above the fold. Ideally for smaller influencers, you'll want your link to be the only link. ## [09:10 - 11:25]

of offer. For example, a free gift with purchase. For Sharnamma, Brandon says that they love the buy one get one or Bogo offer or bundle offers for a higher AOV. However you want the influencer to include some scarcity into the offer to help solicit a direct response like limited time offer for my audience only. One of the main reasons I wanted to use Sharnamma as a case study outside of the fact that they're such open, honest, smart guys is their YouTube funnel. Sharnamma creates custom landers that are geared toward that influencer with their logo, language and style. This way it still feels platform native like they barely left their YouTube channel and helps to leverage their trust and relationship with the audience that step further.
